be accounted for as government property and must be coordinated to the
GSO and MAO for its proper recording in the books of the LGU.

through purchase and donation; and

donated items upon receipt of appropriate documents evidencing transfer of
donations to the LGU.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) direct the officials concerned to ensure that the agency utilize the 20% DF in accordance with the implementing rules and regulations set forth in DILG memorandum Circular No. 2017-1 to ensure that funds are appropriated only for eligible/allowable projects.  Hence, we suggested that the:

a)	subsequent disbursement for the construction of PUV Central Terminal be charged to the appropriation of the Municipal Engineer’s Office (MEO) under General Fund.  A supplemental budget may be enacted for funding purposes, if warranted; and

b)	 financial transactions should be scrutinized carefully to ensure that only items that are directly associated in the implementation of developmental projects are properly charged to the 20% DF to maximize its utilization for more beneficial purpose.
We also recommended that the 20% DF is optimally utilized to attain the desired socio-economic development and environmental outcomes envisioned for the fund.  


Specifically, we suggested that the LCE direct the:

a)	Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to include only projects chargeable to 20% DF that are most viable and can be physically implemented during the fiscal year without any legal impediment; and

b)	the MEO to fast track the implementation of projects that should have been completed in CY 2019.  Also, require the MPDO to submit monitoring report on its status of implementation and use the same as basis in making decision in project implementation strictly in accordance with the approved timetable so that benefits therefrom could be realized the earliest possible time.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) require the MAO to prepare the BRS for all depository accounts for SEF and TF and furnish copy to the AT.  Also, direct the MAO to record book reconciling items to arrive at the correct Cash in Bank balance.
We also recommended strict compliance with Section 3.2 of COA Circular 96-011 and Section 74 of PD No. 1445.

We recommended that the LCE require the MA and the MT to reconcile regularly their respective books/records to detect/identify immediately abnormal account balances.  Likewise, require the MA to effect the necessary adjusting journal entries for the negative balances totaling ₱208,430 in the GF and SEF books to reflect their correct balances.
We also recommended that the MA comply strictly with Section 111 of PD No. 1445, Section 4.1 and Annex B of COA Circular 2015-009.

We recommended that the MAO provide depreciation for all depreciable PPEs and to record adjustments both to the Accumulated Depreciation and Government Equity accounts.
We also recommended strict compliance with PPSAS 17 and Section 2.1 of COA Circular Letter No. 2004-003.

We recommended that the LCE require:
all concerned officials and employees to liquidate their outstanding CAs as of December 31, 2020;
the MA to:
submit to COA the status on the action taken by the LGU to the unliquidated CAs of those separated from service, deceased and also those of the un-identifed accountable officers totaling ₱97,263; and
send demand letters to those employees who are active in service with unliquidated CAs totaling ₱165,422;
We further recommended that the LCE request formally for authority to write-off dormant unliquidated CAs aging 10 years and more totaling ₱68,839 and submit the same to COA Audit Team Leader and/or Supervising Auditor.  In this connection, we recommended that the LCE direct the MA to prepare an aging of unliquidated CAs to support the request for write-off, indicating in the remarks column the existence of the applicable conditions as in indicated on Section 7.4 of COA Circular No. 2016-005 dated December 19, 2016; and
all concerned officials and employees strict compliance with Sections 4.1.2, 4.1.3 and 5.1.3 of COA Circular No. 197-002.

We recommended that the LCE instruct:
the MDRRMC to plan, identify and incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the list of projects and activities to be charged against the unexpended LFRRMF of previous years to make the Investment Plan more comprehensive and integrated for easy monitoring of project implementation and fund utilization; and
the MA to disclose the amount and details of the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF in the Notes to FS.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the:
MPDO to:
reassess the viability of PPAs which had been programmed to be implemented in previous years and consider re-appropriating the corresponding funds;
during planning and preparation of AIP, identify and prioritize the development projects that could be viably implemented to maximize fund utilization; and
submit to COA the Accomplishment Report vis-à-vis 20% DF Plan indicating the status of their implementation; and
Municipal Engineer to fast track the implementation and completion of developmental PPAs for the benefit of the LGU’s constituents.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the BAC Chairman to submit proof of compliance with the requirement on price negotiation in accordance with Section 5 of the GPPB Circular 01-2020.
We also recommended that Management sign or approve contracts or Purchase Orders before commencement of work in accordance with Section 3.5 of the same Circular.

We recommended that the LCE direct the MSWDO to:
facilitate the complete filling-up of the SAC forms of all paid beneficiaries;
re-determine/identify the ineligible beneficiaries and apply to them the appropriate course of action cited under Section VIII-B(6.1) of DSWD MC No. 09; and
ensure strict compliance with DSWD-DOLE-DTI-DA-DOF-DBM-DILG JMC No. 1 and DSWD MC No. 09, both Series of 2020.
We recommended that the LCE require the MT and MA to comply strictly with Item 2.6(a), Chapter 2, Part II of the BOM for LGUs in determining available funds for supplemental budgets.

We recommended that the LCE instruct the MA to remit to the BIR, GSIS, Pag-IBIG and PhilHealth the total amount of ₱0.88 million and to comply strictly with Section 2.58A.2a of Revenue Regulations No. 2-98, Section 6.(b), Rule II, of RA 8291, Section 3.6 Rule II, of RA 8291, Republic Act No. 7742 and Section 20 (b), Rule III, IRR of National Health Insurance Act of 1995.
We recommended that the LCE require all concerned personnel to comply with preparation of records/reports prescribed under COA Circular 2015-008.

c. Institute the appropriate legal actions/sanctions against those erring accountable officers/ employees for continued non-compliance with the existing laws, rules and regulations on the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, if warranted.

We recommended that Report on Physical Count of PPE should be submitted to COA pursuant to Section 124 of NGAS Manual. Also, management should require that Accounting Office and the PGSO to maintain complete and updated Subsidiary Ledger/Property Ledger Card and Property Cards in accordance with the Manual on NGAS.

We recommended that Management require the General Services Officer to:
a. Facilitate the tagging of all assets of the LGU;
b. Ensure that property numbers in the RPCPPE are the same number in the property tags and ARE; and
c. Tag PPE showing complete/required information as above enumerated.

We also recommended that the GSO Designate ensure that no PPE are distributed to its end-user without the property tags and to ensure completeness of information in the property tags such as, tag inventory number, property number, serial number of the property, description and other salient information.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Local Planning and Development Officer as well as the GFPS Technical Working Group to facilitate the establishment of GAD Database. We also recommended that the Management ensure that GAD programs, plans and policies are gender-responsive.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Local DRRM Officer and the Municipal Accountant to prepare the required report and submit the same to the Office of the Auditor within the prescribed period.

We recommended that the BAC Chairman advise the BAC Secretariat to post the Notices of Award and Notices to Proceed together with the approved contracts for the procurement of good, infrastructure projects and services in the PhilGEPS and agency website within the prescribed period as required under Section 37.1.6 and Section 37.4.2 of the IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the General Services Officer or the person-in-charge of the trip tickets to prepare and submit the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption as required under Section 361.g of the Government Accounting and Auditing Manual (GAAM) Volume I.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the General Services Officer/Supply Officer to furnish COA copies of purchase orders/contracts within the prescribed period for timely evaluation and review.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate 1% of the total budget for Senior Citizens and PWDs. We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive establish the Municipal Council for the Elderly and PWDs so that projects/programs/activities for the elderly and the disabled are identified and implemented.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to analyze and trace the transactions involved and make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts to reconcile the balances.
We recommended that the Municipality cause the immediate closure of LGU existing controlled dumpsite and convert it into a sanitary landfill.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive requires the committee of MSWMB to formulate the 10-year Solid Waste Management Plan of the LGU in compliance with NSWMC, and ensure the efficient implementation of solid waste management of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer and the Municipal Development Coordinator provide the Office of the Auditor with a List of all on-going programs/projects/activities (PPAs) and those that are to be implemented during the year.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive establish the Local Council for the Protection of Children (LCPC) and instruct the Municipal Budget Officer to allocate 1% of the total IRA for the strengthening of the LCPC.

We recommended that the Chief Executive require the former Bids and Awards Committee to submit a written explanation on the observations made and provide a valid and logical reason why this particular procurement would not warrant issuance of a Notice of Disallowance.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the contractors to complete the project as soon as possible and impose applicable sanctions and penalties, if warranted. Submit information on what action was undertaken against the contractor, for failure to complete the projects as scheduled.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the officials concerned to ensure that the 20% of IRA is optimally utilized in the prompt implementation of the programs and projects to achieve the desirable socio economic development as prescribed under DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011.

We recommended that:
1. The Municipal Treasurer’s Office submit to Accounting Office all the disbursements vouchers for cash advances, and the liquidation documents on or before the fifth day of the ensuing month, so as not to cause delay as the MOA provides for the immediate implementation of the SF program without unreasonable delay upon receipt of the fund;
2. The Municipal Accounting Office to check the completeness of entries on the Liquidation Reports and Official Receipts for the refund, and supporting documents submitted by the Accountable Officer, and to assign reference/JEV number in the posting of transactions in the Journal of Cash Disbursements. Strictly comply with the timetable on the submission of accounts as required under existing laws, rules and regulations.
3. The Municipal Accountant prepares the necessary adjusting entries for the errors in recording of liquidations-

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor requires the concerned offices to update and complete the conduct of inventory and to cause the immediate reconciliation between the books and the actual inventory of all PPE owned by the LGU to determine the accuracy and existence of the recorded PPE in the financial statements.

We recommended that the LGU:
1. Submit notarized copy of Memorandum of Agreement with signature of all concerned signatories.
2. Submit copy of Designation of Authorized Person for the project implementation. The position and designation of names of persons whose signatures appeared in the transaction, Ms. Nelia Dulay and Mr. Neal Pastor Boragay.
3. The BAC to explain the discrepancy in the mode of procurement – as stated in the Contract of Agreement and in the Purchase Order, likewise, the delivery period of one hundred days in one contract and August 17, 2015 in the other contract which covered the same transaction.
4. Submit authority of Mr. Neal Pastor Boragay as signatory to the Purchase Request, and as person who received the goods delivered to the LGU.
5. Compute the penalty for late delivery of fifty-four (54) units pedicab covered by delivery receipt dated September 18, 2015 for thirty-one (31) days.

We recommended that the LGU should make representation with the source agency if the above terms can be applied to the project involved, and to whose accounting books the property is to be recorded, and take necessary steps/action to comply with the terms and conditions in the MOA. Likewise recommend submission of copy of list if intended beneficiaries (248) which was submitted to DOLE for the project proposal funding request, and the list of recipients of the 114 pedicab.

We recommended that the Management instruct the Municipal Development Council to strictly include programs and projects chargeable to the 20% Local Development Fund that are specifically embodied in the DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 to ensure the attainment of desirable social, economic and developmental outcomes for the benefit of the constituents.
Require the Municipal Accountant to set-up as the Receivable Account from the PNP the amount of ₱500,000.00 fund transferred to PNP-PRO5 which was among the reported expenditures not proper charges to the 20% EDF. Further follow up from said office the submission of Report of Disbursement duly verified by the Auditor of PNP-PRO5 as recipient of the fund transferred.

We recommended that in the preparation of Annual Procurement Plan and all program of works and matters pertaining to the 20% EDF, the Local Chief Executive requires the signature and involvement of the OIC-MPDO in the absence of the head of the MPDO.

We also recommended to record in the books of the LGU the purchased IT Equipment with supporting Property Ledger Card and Acknowledgement Receipt of Equipment on file with the Property Officer for inclusion in the inventory report.

We recommended that Management provide the oversight agencies copies of the approved Annual GAD Plan and Budget and the prescribed Accomplishment Report on GAD and supporting documents to comply with the requirements of the law and furnish COA copy of the report with stamped received. The cost of its implementation shall be at least five percent (5%) of their total budget.

Likewise, we recommended the Creation and/or Strengthening of the GAD Focal Point System as required by the Magna Carta of Women (MCW) and following the Philippine Commission on Women (PCW) Memorandum Circular 2011-01, that all agencies should have an institutionalized and functional GAD Focal Point System (GFPS).

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant be required the timely submission of the monthly and year-end Financial Statements and other reports/schedules for the General Fund, Special Education Fund and Trust Fund for CY 2015 in accordance with the regulations.
We recommended that LCE as Chairman, Municipal School Board direct the school officials early convening of the Municipal School Board and approval of the Special Education Fund Annual Budget.

We recommended that the Municipal Engineer should closely monitor the implementation of infrastructure projects and make sure that the same were completed within the target date. The LCE should immediately act on reports regarding defects in the implementation of projects such as those not completed within the contract period and recommended the imposition of penalty charges, if warranted.

We recommended that the LCE should require the LGU’s GAD Focal Point System to submit the GAD Plan and Budget and the corresponding GAD Accomplishment Reports for CY 2014 to Philippine Commission on Women and for the ensuing years. Furnish the Commission on Audit copy of the approved GAD Plan and proof of submission to PCW of the GAD 2014 Accomplishment Report.

We recommended to the LCE that all programs, projects and activities under the 20% EDF should be fully and effectively implemented during the year. The MPDC should be directly involved in the implementation of projects and all program of works should bear the concurrence of the MDPC. Strictly observe the proper charging of expenses such as the burial expenses which should be classified as Assistance to Individual in Crisis Situation an activity not classified under the 20% EDP.

Likewise recommended that the Municipal Engineer should not be required/allowed to perform the functions of two (2) major divisions of the LGU such as OIC-MPDC which both involves sensitive functions of implementing and monitoring the major programs and projects of the LGU.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should continue to ensure that the 20% EDF are programmed/utilized in accordance with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (LCE) should require the Municipal Treasurer to properly record transactions in the cashbook and indicate the date in the RCDs when remittances was acknowledged from collectors/tellers. The actual date of deposit should be recorded in the cashbook as indicated in the validated deposit slips. Cashbook entries should be in accordance with the rules and regulations on keeping of accounts.

We recommended that the accounting office should strictly observe the setting up of accounts payable pursuant to NGAS. Only claims with valid supporting documents should be recognized and recorded in the books of accounts and those without valid claimants should be reverted as required in Section 98 of PD 1445 so that funds could be used to support other projects, programs and activities of the municipality.
We recommend to management to adhere strictly to the provisions of Section 325(a) of RA 7160 so as not to exceed the PS limitation and suffer the consequences relative thereto.

We recommended for both the Municipal Treasurer and the Accounting to sit down and reconcile their records in order to come up with corrected and verified balances for all funds. Items uncovered during the reconciliation process should be effected to ensure the correctness of the balance of this particular account in the financial statements.
